html:
[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 次日命副将某率兵往环塔 - 次日 means 'the next day,' 命 means 'order' or 'command,' 副将 refers to a deputy general, 率兵 means 'lead the troops,' and 往环塔 means 'to the Ring Pagoda.' This phrase sets the scene for the military action.
  • 彀弓注矢以待 - 彀弓 means 'draw the bow,' 注矢 means 'load the arrow,' and 以待 means 'in preparation.' This describes the soldiers' readiness.
  • 众目睽睽 - A classical expression meaning 'with many eyes watching,' indicating a public or conspicuous setting.
  • 仰注塔上 - 仰注 means 'look up to,' and 塔上 means 'the top of the pagoda.' This describes the soldiers looking up at the pagoda.
  • 一道红光，瞥如飞电 - 一道红光 means 'a streak of red light,' 瞥如飞电 means 'flickering like lightning.' This vividly describes the appearance of the red light.
  • 数珠已挂于顶 - 数珠 refers to a rosary or prayer beads, 已挂于顶 means 'already hanging on the top.' This indicates that the rosary has been placed on the top of the pagoda.
  • 万弩齐发 - 万弩 means 'thousands of crossbows,' 齐发 means 'fired simultaneously.' This describes the simultaneous firing of crossbows.
  • 渺然如捕风影焉 - 渺然 means 'vaguely' or 'dimly,' 如捕风影 means 'like catching a shadow,' 焉 is a particle that adds a sense of finality. This suggests that the target was elusive and difficult to hit.
  • 令健卒梯而登 - 令 means 'order,' 健卒 means 'strong soldiers,' 梯而登 means 'climb using a ladder.' This describes the soldiers climbing the pagoda.
  • 珠上系书一封 - 珠上 means 'on the rosary,' 系书一封 means 'tied with a letter.' This indicates that a letter was attached to the rosary.
  • 题曰“空空儿手缄” - 题曰 means 'inscribed as,' 空空儿 is a nickname, 手缄 means 'handwritten seal.' This introduces the sender of the letter.
  • 莅任以来 - 莅任 means 'since taking office,' 以来 means 'since.' This indicates the period of time since the official took office.
  • 挟威以扰士民 - 挟威 means 'with power,' 以扰士民 means 'to disturb the scholars and common people.' This describes the official's use of power to disturb the people.
  • 挟术以欺君上 - 挟术 means 'with cunning,' 以欺君上 means 'to deceive the ruler.' This describes the official's use of cunning to deceive the ruler.
  • 挟势以辱长吏 - 挟势 means 'with authority,' 以辱长吏 means 'to humiliate the local officials.' This describes the official's use of authority to humiliate local officials.
  • 诇察纵武弁 - 诇察 means 'to spy on,' 纵武弁 means 'to allow soldiers to act freely.' This describes the official's spying and allowing soldiers to act without restraint.
  • 罗织为腹心 - 罗织 means 'to fabricate charges,' 为腹心 means 'as close advisors.' This describes the official's fabrication of charges against close advisors.
  • 凌辱称孤立 - 凌辱 means 'to bully,' 称孤立 means 'claiming isolation.' This describes the official's bullying and claiming isolation.
  • 济贪以酷 - 济贪 means 'to further greed,' 以酷 means 'with cruelty.' This describes the official's furthering greed with cruelty.
  • 行诈以权 - 行诈 means 'to practice deceit,' 以权 means 'with power.' This describes the official's practicing deceit with power.
  • 身荷封疆之任 - 身荷 means 'shouldering,' 封疆之任 means 'the responsibility of border defense.' This describes the official's responsibility for border defense.
  • 心怀鬼蜮之谋 - 心怀 means 'in the heart,' 鬼蜮之谋 means 'plots of the underworld.' This describes the official's malicious intentions.
  • 绅士无所控 - 绅士 means 'gentlemen' or 'scholars,' 无所控 means 'having no recourse.' This describes the scholars having no way to complain.
  • 科道不敢纠 - 科道 refers to censors and remonstrators, 不敢纠 means 'dare not criticize.' This describes the censors and remonstrators not daring to criticize.
  • 取公此物，聊用示警 - 取公此物 means 'to take this item from you,' 聊用示警 means 'just to serve as a warning.' This describes the intention behind taking the item.
  • 若不速图悛改 - 若不 means 'if not,' 速图 means 'quickly plan,' 悛改 means 'to repent and reform.' This describes the condition for avoiding punishment.
  • 仍蹈前愆 - 仍 means 'still,' 蹈 means 'to tread,' 前愆 means 'past faults.' This describes continuing past mistakes.
  • 即当取公首级 - 即当 means 'will immediately,' 取公首级 means 'take your head.' This describes the consequence of not reforming.
  • 以为为大吏者戒 - 以为 means 'to serve as,' 为大吏者 means 'for high-ranking officials,' 戒 means 'warning.' This describes the purpose of the warning.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 环塔 - Refers to a specific pagoda, likely a significant landmark in the region.
  • 空空儿 - A nickname, possibly referring to a specific individual or a symbolic figure.
  • 封疆之任 - Refers to the responsibility of border defense, a significant role in ancient Chinese governance.
  • 鬼蜮之谋 - Refers to malicious plots, often associated with deceit and treachery.
  • 科道 - Refers to the censorial and remonstratory officials in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 绅士 - Refers to the educated elite, including scholars and local gentry.

Proper Nouns

  • 环塔 - A specific pagoda.
  • 空空儿 - A nickname.

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 毛骨俱悚 - Vivid expression meaning 'to be so frightened that one's hair stands on end and one shivers,' conveying an intense emotional reaction.
  • 贪暴从此稍戢焉 - Literally 'greed and violence were somewhat restrained from then on,' suggesting a change in behavior due to fear or other factors.
  • 忽浓云四布 - Means 'suddenly dark clouds spread all around,' describing a sudden and dramatic change in weather.
  • 风雨交作 - Means 'wind and rain mix together,' indicating a violent storm.
  • 天黑如漆 - Simile meaning 'the sky was as black as ink,' emphasizing the darkness.
  • 不辨东西 - Means 'unable to distinguish east from west,' indicating complete disorientation.
  • 舟子大怖，进退失措 - Means 'the boatman was greatly terrified and at a loss whether to advance or retreat,' describing a state of panic and confusion.
  • 榜徨间 - Means 'in a state of bewilderment,' indicating a moment of indecision.
  • 倏睹林薄中燐火一点 - Means 'suddenly saw a single point of phosphorescent light in the dense undergrowth,' describing a sudden and unexpected sight.
  • 光巨于灯 - Means 'the light was larger than a lamp,' emphasizing the intensity and size of the light.
  • 渐移近岸 - Means 'gradually moved closer to the shore,' describing the movement of the light.
  • 闪影晶莹 - Means 'shining with a clear, brilliant light,' emphasizing the quality of the light.
  • 照水如白昼 - Means 'illuminated the water as if it were daytime,' indicating the brightness of the light.
  • 舟行则燐亦行 - Means 'as the boat moved, so did the phosphorescent light,' suggesting a connection between the boat and the light.
  • 如为导引者 - Means 'as if it were guiding the way,' indicating the light's role as a guide.
  • 直至村中大虹桥 - Means 'until it reached the large stone bridge in the village,' specifying the destination of the light.
  • 光始不见 - Means 'the light then disappeared,' indicating the end of the light's presence.
  • 计所照水程已三十余里矣 - Means 'calculating the distance illuminated by the light, it was over thirty miles,' emphasizing the extent of the light's reach.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 桐乡 - Place name in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 三家村 - Fictional or unspecified small village, often used in literature to represent a typical rural setting.
  • 郡中 - Refers to the capital city or administrative center of a region.
  • 永兴堰 - Specific location, likely a ferry or crossing point.
  • 鬼灯 - Ghostly light, often associated with supernatural phenomena.
  • 徐小山 - Historical or fictional figure, possibly known for his skills in divination.
  • 小山之善报 - Refers to a good omen or positive outcome for Xiao Shan.

Proper Nouns

  • 太保 - High-ranking official, often a tutor or advisor.
  • 徐小山 - Historical or fictional figure, possibly known for his skills in divination.
  • 永兴堰 - Specific location, likely a ferry or crossing point.
  • 鬼灯 - Ghostly light, often associated with supernatural phenomena.
  • 桐乡 - Place name in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 三家村 - Fictional or unspecified small village.
  • 郡中 - Capital city or administrative center of a region.
  • 大虹桥 - Specific stone bridge in the village.
  • 可石 - Source of the story, possibly a historian or chronicler.

[212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - A formal way of introducing a commentary or opinion, often used in historical or literary texts.
  • 先君 - Refers to one's father or an ancestor; here, likely the author's father.
  • 自荆入蜀 - Means 'from Jing to Shu,' referring to a journey from the Jing region (modern-day Hubei) to the Shu region (modern-day Sichuan).
  • 应韦南康辟命 - Means 'responded to the invitation of Wei Nankang,' where Wei Nankang was a high-ranking official.
  • 后韦薨 - Means 'after Wei died,' where '薨' is a respectful term for the death of a high-ranking person.
  • 贼辟知留后 - Means 'the usurper took control as the acting governor,' where '贼' implies a usurper or rebel.
  • 旧与辟不合 - Means 'had previously disagreed with the usurper.'
  • 连夜离县 - Means 'left the county at night,' indicating a hurried departure.
  • 不令诸县官离县 - Means 'ordered all county officials not to leave their posts.'
  • 初意县吏迎候 - Means 'initially thought it was the county officials coming to greet.'
  • 百步为导 - Means 'with two torches leading the way for a hundred steps.'
  • 高下远近不差 - Means 'without any deviation in height, distance, or direction.'
  • 欲及县郭方灭 - Means 'just as they were about to reach the county gates, they disappeared.'
  • 念《金刚经》已五六年 - Means 'had been reciting the *Diamond Sutra* for five or six years.'
  • 向之导火，乃经所著迹 - Means 'the torches that guided the way were actually traces left by the sutra.'
  • 小山素亦未尝持经咒 - Means 'Xiao Shan (the author) had never recited sutras or mantras before.'
  • 未必果为诵经所致也 - Means 'it may not have been caused by reciting the sutra.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贞元中 - Refers to the reign period of Emperor Dezong of Tang (780–805 AD).
  • 先君 - Refers to the author's father, who is unnamed but played a significant role in the narrative.
  • 韦南康 - Refers to Wei Nankang, a high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 贼辟 - Refers to a usurper who took control of the region after Wei Nankang's death.
  • 《金刚经》 - Refers to the *Diamond Sutra*, a central text in Mahayana Buddhism.
  • 小山 - The author's self-reference, likely a pseudonym or pen name.

Proper Nouns

  • 贞元 - The reign period of Emperor Dezong of Tang (780–805 AD).
  • 先君 - The author's father, whose name is not provided.
  • 韦南康 - Wei Nankang, a high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 贼辟 - The usurper who took control of the region.
  • 《金刚经》 - The *Diamond Sutra*, a central text in Mahayana Buddhism.
  • 小山 - The author's self-reference, likely a pseudonym or pen name.
  • 段成式 - The author of *The Unusual Events of the Diamond Sutra* (《金刚经鸩异》), a historical figure known for his writings on unusual events.

[213]

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • The passage reflects a common structure in classical Chinese texts, where events are described in a chronological and formal manner, often used in official records or historical accounts.

[214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 性好外 - Means 'has a fondness for the outside' or 'is inclined to be outgoing,' describing Zhou's personality.
  • 与周寝食必俱 - Means 'always sleeps and eats together with Zhou,' indicating a close relationship.
  • 情好颇笃 - Means 'has a deep affection,' describing the strong bond between Zhou and Zhang.
  • 呼为张毛弟 - Means 'calls him Zhang Maodi,' referring to the nickname given to the boy.
  • 未几张死 - Means 'not long after Zhang died,' indicating the timing of Zhang's death.
  • 瘗于秦观峰侧 - Means 'buried on the side of Qiguan Peak,' describing the location of Zhang's burial.
  • 遥见残雪中 - Means 'far off in the snow,' setting the scene for the hunter's encounter.
  • 一兔方与狐交 - Means 'a rabbit was just mating with a fox,' describing the unusual scene observed by the hunter.
  • 逐而射之 - Means 'chased and shot it,' describing the hunter's action.
  • 中其尻 - Means 'hit its buttocks,' describing the location of the arrow's impact.
  • 兔带箭而逃 - Means 'the rabbit fled with the arrow,' describing the rabbit's reaction.
  • 入一破棺中 - Means 'into an old coffin,' describing the rabbit's unusual destination.
  • 即之，竟不见 - Means 'when they approached, they saw nothing,' describing the result of the search.
  • 但存一枯骸而已 - Means 'only a dried skeleton remained,' describing what was found in the coffin.
  • 今固应与狐魅为偶矣 - Means 'it must now be paired with a fox spirit,' suggesting the supernatural transformation.
  • 猎者悚然 - Means 'the hunter was greatly alarmed,' describing the hunter's reaction.
  • 投弓矢而返 - Means 'threw away his bow and arrows and returned,' describing the hunter's subsequent action.
  • 自是遂不复猎 - Means 'from then on, he never hunted again,' describing the hunter's decision.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 泰安 - Refers to Taian, a city in Shandong Province, China.
  • 富室 - Refers to a wealthy household.
  • 娈童 - Refers to a young male lover or concubine, often associated with homosexual relationships in historical contexts.
  • 秦观峰 - Refers to Qiguan Peak, a specific location in the area.
  • 狐魅 - Refers to a fox spirit, often associated with supernatural beings in Chinese folklore.

Proper Nouns

  • 周某 - Refers to a wealthy man named Zhou.
  • 张毛弟 - Refers to the nickname given to the boy.
  • 秦观峰 - Refers to Qiguan Peak.
  • 猎者 - Refers to the hunter.

[215]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 素以操行自许 - Always considered his conduct as his own boast, meaning he prided himself on his moral integrity.
  • 人以鲁男子为铁石心肠 - People consider men like Lu Manzi to have hearts of stone, implying that they are emotionally detached.
  • 吾窃笑其柔情未断 - I secretly laugh at their unbroken tenderness, suggesting a subtle criticism of those who claim to be emotionally detached but still show signs of sentimentality.
  • 馆于富室赵氏 - Taught in the wealthy household of Zhao, indicating that the father worked as a private tutor for a wealthy family.
  • 课艺未完 - Lessons not yet finished, referring to the completion of academic tasks.
  • 关帝像自帧中冉冉而下 - The image of Guan Di descended slowly from the frame, describing a supernatural event where the image of Guan Di moved.
  • 索观其草稿 - Requested to see his draft, meaning the deity asked to review the students' work.
  • 点窜讲解 - Corrected and explained, indicating that the deity provided corrections and explanations.
  • 精妙入神 - Exquisite and divine, describing the quality of the corrections and explanations.
  • 仍归画上 - Returned to the painting, indicating that the deity's image returned to its original position.
  • 缮录 - Neatly copied, meaning to carefully rewrite or copy the text.
  • 托故辞归 - Excused himself and returned home, indicating that the father left his position under pretense.
  • 此画妖也 - This is a painting demon, suggesting that the father believed the incident was caused by a supernatural entity.
  • 妖由人兴 - Demons arise from human actions, implying that supernatural phenomena are often the result of human behavior.
  • 几见邪魅而能惑正人端士者 - How often do we see demons that can deceive virtuous and upright scholars? questioning the likelihood of demons deceiving morally upright individuals.
  • 父慖怯不敢复留 - My father is timid and does not dare to stay, indicating that the father was too fearful to remain in his position.
  • 儿请往代摄其事 - I will go in my father's place and take over the duties, showing the son's willingness to take over his father's responsibilities.
  • 看此妖敢来魅我否 - See if this demon dares to deceive me, expressing the son's confidence in his moral integrity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 彰德 - A region in Henan Province, China.
  • 关帝 - Refers to Guan Yu (关公), a famous general and deified figure in Chinese culture, often associated with loyalty and righteousness.
  • 赵氏 - Refers to the wealthy family where the father taught.
  • 鲁男子 - A reference to a historical figure known for his stoic demeanor, often used to describe someone who appears emotionally detached.

Proper Nouns

  • 马宏谟 - The name of the protagonist.
  • 若虚 - The father of 马宏谟.
  • 赵氏 - The wealthy family where the father taught.
  • 关帝 - The deity Guan Yu.

[216]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 后值重五 - After the fifth day of the fifth month (Double Fifth Festival).
  • 塾徒皆散 - All the students in the private school have dispersed.
  • 旅窗枯坐 - Sitting alone in the study room.
  • 不禁思乡之感 - Cannot help but feel homesick.
  • 信步至后园 - Wandered aimlessly to the back garden.
  • 其中亭屋颇极幽邃 - The pavilions and buildings in it are very secluded and deep.
  • 远望东畔一小池 - Looking far to the east side, there is a small pond.
  • 荷花已开 - The lotus flowers have already bloomed.
  • 急趋之 - Rushed to it.
  • 池上有楼翼然 - There is a pavilion over the pond, like a bird spreading its wings.
  • 意将登览以豁幽怀 - Intending to climb up and enjoy the view to dispel my melancholy.
  • 而扃鐍甚固 - But the door was locked very tightly.
  • 正徬徨间 - Just as he was hesitating.
  • 忽双扉砑然自启 - Suddenly, the two doors opened by themselves.
  • 一二十许丽人迎门 - About twenty beautiful women greeted him at the door.
  • 瓠犀微露 - Their teeth were slightly visible.
  • 以手相招 - They waved their hands to invite him.
  • 风流靡曼 - Graceful and alluring.
  • 世间无其匹也 - There is no match for them in the world.
  • 生对方久旷 - The protagonist had been long without a partner.
  • 乘兴从入 - He followed them in, following his impulse.
  • 女转身上楼 - The women turned and went upstairs.
  • 生亦抬级随上 - The protagonist also climbed up the stairs to follow them.
  • 直前拥抱 - He embraced them directly.
  • 此女忽变一厉鬼 - Suddenly, this woman turned into a fierce ghost.
  • 被发相攫 - Her hair was disheveled, and she grabbed him.
  • 骇绝急奔 - He was terrified and ran desperately.
  • 及梯而仆 - He fell down the stairs.
  • 忽头上砰然作声 - Suddenly, there was a loud noise on his head.
  • 其左足已为楼扉所压 - His left foot was crushed by the door.
  • 而身倒悬干下 - And his body was hanging upside down.
  • 大嗥 - He screamed loudly.
  • 群集救之 - The people gathered to save him.
  • 竭力启扉不可得 - They tried their best to open the door but couldn't.
  • 其主人仰视久之 - His host looked up at it for a long time.
  • 心知其异 - He knew it was strange.
  • 急出呼一犬至 - He quickly called a dog.
  • 取械击之 - And took a weapon to hit it.
  • 犬嗥声大作 - The dog barked loudly.
  • 而生足脱然出矣 - And the protagonist's foot came out easily.
  • 扶掖至斋中 - They helped him to the study room.
  • 细询其状 - They asked him in detail about what happened.
  • 生此时惊魂丧惘 - The protagonist was still shocked and bewildered.
  • 不觉吐实 - He unconsciously told the truth.
  • 主人从旁笑曰 - The host laughed and said.
  • 先生不知，此楼向为狐魅所窟 - You didn't know that this pavilion has been a den for fox spirits.
  • 故终年常扃闭不启 - So it is always locked and never opened.
  • 不意先生乃亦为狐魅所惑也 - I didn't expect that you would also be deceived by the fox spirits.
  • 众皆粲然 - Everyone laughed.
  • 生汗颜，不能仰视 - The protagonist blushed and couldn't look up.
  • 翌日，乃以足蹇辞主人归 - The next day, he excused himself with a lame foot and returned home.
  • 竟不复已 - And he never returned.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 重五 - The Double Fifth Festival, also known as the Dragon Boat Festival, celebrated on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month.
  • 塾徒 - Students in a private school, indicating a traditional educational setting.
  • 旅窗 - A study room or private study, often associated with scholarly activities.
  • 后园 - A back garden, often a place of relaxation and contemplation.
  • 亭屋 - Pavilions and buildings, often found in gardens and used for leisure and social gatherings.
  • 荷花 - Lotus flowers, symbolizing purity and beauty in Chinese culture.
  • 狐魅 - Fox spirits, often associated with mischief and deception in Chinese folklore.

[217]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - A phrase indicating that the following text is a commentary or reflection by an external historian or scholar.
  • 色厉而内荏 - Classical idiom meaning 'seemingly fierce but actually weak inside,' describing a person who appears stern but lacks inner strength.
  • 微生高之直 - Refers to Wei Shengao, a historical figure known for his straightforwardness.
  • 张君瑞之远色 - Refers to Zhang Junrui, a historical figure known for his avoidance of lustful desires.
  • 盗取虚声 - Literally means 'to steal a false reputation,' referring to gaining fame through superficial or insincere means.
  • 卒也 - Means 'in the end' or 'finally,' marking a transition to the conclusion of the narrative.
  • 尤物 - Refers to a beautiful or alluring woman, often with a connotation of temptation.
  • 迷乱失次 - Means 'confused and disordered,' describing a state of mental or emotional chaos.
  • 忽现变相 - Literally means 'suddenly appeared in a transformed state,' referring to a sudden and unexpected change.
  • 伤其足 - Means 'injured his foot,' indicating a physical harm.
  • 不至失足 - Means 'did not lose his footing,' suggesting that despite the injury, he did not fall completely.
  • 失足过半矣 - Means 'has already lost his footing more than half,' indicating that he has already succumbed to a significant degree.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - Refers to an external historian or commentator, often used in classical texts to distinguish between primary and secondary sources.
  • 茅山道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest from Mount Mao, a famous Taoist site in China.
  • 戴旷如 - The name of a character, likely a historical or fictional figure, who practices medicine.
  • 业疡医 - Means 'practices ulcer treatment,' referring to a specific medical specialty.
  • 门可张罗 - A phrase meaning 'the door is open for visitors,' indicating that the person is well-known or respected.
  • 游方道士 - Refers to a wandering Taoist priest, often associated with spiritual or mystical practices.
  • 葛巾布袍 - Describes the attire of a Taoist priest, typically a simple and humble garment.
  • 造门化斋 - Means 'came to the door to beg for food,' a common practice of wandering monks or priests.
  • 自茅山来 - Indicates that the Taoist priest comes from Mount Mao, emphasizing his spiritual background.
  • 皂囊 - Refers to a black bag, often used to carry important items.
  • 丹方 - Means 'formula for elixir,' referring to a recipe for a magical or medicinal substance.
  • 孙真人 - Refers to Sun Simiao, a famous Taoist physician and alchemist.
  • 老龙 - Refers to an ancient dragon, often associated with Taoist legends and mystical powers.
  • 方外交 - Means 'informal friend,' indicating a relationship that is not strictly formal or official.

Proper Nouns

  • 外史氏 - An external historian or commentator.
  • 马生 - A character, likely a historical or fictional figure.
  • 微生高 - A historical figure known for his straightforwardness.
  • 张君瑞 - A historical figure known for his avoidance of lustful desires.
  • 戴旷如 - The name of a character, likely a historical or fictional figure.
  • 戴家山村 - The village where Dai Kuangru lives.
  • 茅山道士 - A Taoist priest from Mount Mao.
  • 孙真人 - Sun Simiao, a famous Taoist physician and alchemist.
  • 老龙 - An ancient dragon, often associated with Taoist legends and mystical powers.

[218]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 后数日复至，谓之曰 - A formal expression meaning 'a few days later, he returned and said to him.'
  • 前所授方虽妙，然须辨症施治 - Means 'the formula previously given was indeed wonderful, but it requires distinguishing the symptoms and applying the treatment accordingly.'
  • 仆尚有小术，君固欲得之乎 - Means 'I still have a small trick; are you indeed eager to learn it?'
  • 戴大喜，请教 - Means 'Dai was very pleased and asked to be taught.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 居二日又死 - Means 'after two days, she died again.' Indicates a repeated death, suggesting a supernatural or unusual circumstance.
  • 死一日复苏 - Means 'she revived after one day.' Describes a miraculous recovery after death.
  • 非复向者去路 - Means 'not the same path as before.' Suggests a change in the direction or nature of the journey.
  • 阴风惨淡 - Describes a bleak and gloomy atmosphere with cold winds, often used to evoke a sense of foreboding or sorrow.
  • 天地异色 - Means 'the sky and earth have a different color,' indicating a change in the natural environment, possibly due to a supernatural event.
  • 皓首茧袍者 - Refers to an elderly man wearing a coarse robe, suggesting a wise or experienced figure.
  • 见之讶然 - Means 'he was surprised to see her,' indicating a reaction of astonishment.
  • 汝阳数未尽 - Means 'your life has not yet ended,' suggesting that the person's lifespan has not been fully exhausted.
  • 宜遽返 - Means 'you should return immediately,' indicating an urgent need to return home.
  • 尸已腐矣 - Means 'your body will already be rotten,' emphasizing the urgency of returning.
  • 向鬼役缓颊数语 - Means 'he spoke a few words to the ghostly officials to plead for her,' indicating an attempt to persuade the supernatural beings.
  • 释之而去 - Means 'they released her and left,' indicating the release from captivity.
  • 神气渐夷 - Means 'her spirit gradually became calm,' indicating a recovery of mental and physical well-being.
  • 咸谓其终不应死也 - Means 'everyone believed she should not have died in the end,' indicating a consensus that her death was unexpected.
  • 翌日竟死 - Means 'she died the next day,' indicating a final and definitive death.
  • 自是不复苏 - Means 'she never revived again,' indicating the finality of her death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鬼役 - Refers to ghostly officials or spirits who enforce supernatural laws, often found in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 尸已腐矣 - The concept of a decaying body is a common theme in Chinese literature, often used to emphasize the urgency of action or the inevitability of death.
  • 神气渐夷 - The idea of a calm and peaceful spirit is a common theme in Chinese philosophy, particularly in Confucian and Daoist texts.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 居二日又死，死一日复苏 - This sequence of events is compact and can be read as a series of rapid changes, emphasizing the miraculous nature of the recovery.
  • 中途遇一皓首茧袍者 - This phrase introduces a new character and sets up the interaction with the elderly man.
  • 汝阳数未尽。宜遽返，再迟则尸已腐矣 - This clause is a direct instruction from the elderly man, emphasizing the urgency of returning home.
  • 因向鬼役缓颊数语，鬼役释之而去，乃得还家焉 - This sequence of actions is compact and must be read as a series of events leading to the release and return home.
  • 进以汤药，神气渐夷 - This phrase describes the care given to the woman and her gradual recovery.
  • 咸谓其终不应死也 - This clause summarizes the community's belief about her survival.
  • 无何，病复剧，翌日竟死 - This sequence of events describes the recurrence of illness and the final death.
  • 自是不复苏 - This final clause emphasizes the finality of her death.

  • 曩时羞颜所不能及 - What I was too shy to do before.
  • 别离在即 - Parting is imminent.

  • 意将寻女 - Means 'intending to look for his daughter,' expressing a purposeful intention.

  • 忽见羽幢绣幌 - Means 'suddenly saw a banner with embroidered curtains,' describing a sudden and striking visual encounter.

  • 一女子皓腕搴帘睇视 - Means 'a woman with white wrists pulled back the curtain and looked at him,' describing a specific action and appearance.

  • 泣诉相觅之故 - Means 'told with tears about the reason for searching,' expressing an emotional and detailed explanation.

  • 妾以郎病未愈 - Means 'I thought your illness had not yet healed,' providing a reason for her visit.

  • 故复纡道来视近已安否 - Means 'So I took a detour to see if you were well recently,' explaining the reason for her visit.
  • 今有一喜信报君知 - Means 'Now I have good news to tell you,' introducing new information.
  • 昨闻真官韩愈奏 - Means 'Yesterday I heard that the true official Han Yu reported,' indicating a recent event.
  • 今番考试不公已极 - Means 'This time the examination was extremely unfair,' criticizing the fairness of the exam.
  • 来岁恩科 - Refers to 'next year's favor-granting examination,' referring to a special exam.
  • 须先将试官甄别 - Means 'must first screen the examiners,' suggesting a need for reform.
  • 庶免屈抑人才 - Means 'to prevent the suppression of talented people,' expressing the goal of reform.
  • 帝即以命愈 - Means 'the emperor immediately appointed Han Yu,' indicating a royal decision.
  • 愈以顺天犹为人文渊薮 - Means 'Han Yu believed that Shuntian (Beijing) was still a hub of culture and talent,' providing a rationale for his choice.
  • 拟将以汪廷珍为顺天正考官 - Means 'he proposed to appoint Wang Tingzhen as the chief examiner of Shuntian,' indicating a specific recommendation.
  • 此人素为君知己 - Means 'This person has always been your friend,' expressing a personal connection.
  • 君若赴试，自应针芥无差 - Means 'If you take the exam, you should naturally fit well,' expressing confidence in the outcome.
  • 遂拔髻上一玳瑁簪与之 - Means 'She then pulled out a tortoise-shell hairpin from her hair and gave it to him,' describing a specific action.
  • 妾此时将赴南岳夫人宴 - Means 'I am now going to attend a banquet hosted by the Lady of Nanyue,' providing a reason for her departure.
  • 不能久留 - Means 'cannot stay long,' indicating a limited time.

  • 及夜半，疯子脱身上破衲与张曰 - Madman giving his robe to Zhang; '及夜半' means 'by midnight', '脱身' means 'to take off', '破衲' means 'tattered robe', and '与张曰' means 'gave to Zhang and said'.
  • 服之可御寒 - Instruction to wear the robe to ward off the cold; '服之' means 'wear it', and '御寒' means 'to ward off the cold'.

  • 居数年，二人共往访笑和尚 - Visit to the laughing monk after several years; '居数年' means 'after several years', '二人' means 'the two of them', '共往' means 'together went', and '访' means 'to visit'.
  • 和尚迎笑曰 - Enthusiastic greeting; '迎笑' means 'greeted with laughter', and '曰' means 'said'.

  • 抱张颈狂笑 - Embracing Zhang's neck and laughing wildly; '抱张颈' means 'embraced Zhang's neck', and '狂笑' means 'laughed wildly'.
  • 声如鸾凤，使人心魄俱摇 - Captivating voice; '声如鸾凤' means 'voice like a phoenix', and '使人心魄俱摇' means 'made one's heart and soul tremble'.
  • 疯子从旁骂曰 - Madman's reaction; '从旁' means 'from the side', '骂' means 'scolded', and '曰' means 'said'.
  • 憨和尚，汝笑至今日犹以为未足耶 - Scolding of the monk; '憨和尚' means 'simple monk', '笑至今日' means 'laughed until today', '犹以为未足' means 'still think it's not enough', and '耶' is an interrogative particle.
  • 和尚膜拜曰 - Humble response; '膜拜' means 'prostrated in reverence', and '曰' means 'said'.
  • 吾知罪矣。然老僧不死，笑终不可止也 - Confession and justification; '吾知罪矣' means 'I know I have sinned', '然' means 'but', '老僧不死' means 'the old monk will not die', and '笑终不可止也' means 'laughing cannot be stopped'.
  • 竭力忍笑上床，趺坐而逝 - Final moments; '竭力忍笑' means 'struggled to hold back laughter', '上床' means 'onto the bed', '趺坐' means 'cross-legged', and '逝' means 'died'.
  • 徐笑顾张曰 - Final words; '徐笑' means 'slowly laughed', '顾' means 'looked at', and '曰' means 'said'.
  • 可以行矣 - Final instruction; '可以' means 'can', '行' means 'go', and '矣' is a particle indicating completion.
  • 携手出门，忽不见 - Departure; '携手' means 'holding hands', '出门' means 'out of the door', and '忽不见' means 'suddenly disappeared'.
  • 仙乎仙乎 - Supernatural departure; '仙乎' means 'immortal', and the repetition emphasizes the supernatural nature of the event.
  • 或谓笑和尚生长太平，其以乐死也，自非生逢离乱者所可拟 - Commentary on the monk's life and death; '或谓' means 'some say', '生长太平' means 'grew up in peaceful times', '其以乐死也' means 'he died in joy', '自非' means 'not', '生逢离乱者' means 'those born in times of chaos', and '所可拟' means 'can be compared'.
  • 然观其临逝数语，乌知其中无长歌当哭时耶 - Suggestion that the monk's laughter might have been a form of mourning; '然' means 'but', '观其临逝数语' means 'see his final words', '乌知' means 'how can one know', '其中' means 'in it', '无长歌当哭时' means 'no time for a long song to mourn', and '耶' is an interrogative particle.
  • 此笑和尚之溺于笑，殆犹醉和尚之溺于饮而意不在饮也，则其笑亦可传已 - Comparison of the monk's laughter to a drunk monk's drinking; '此笑和尚之溺于笑' means 'this monk's obsession with laughter', '殆' means 'probably', '犹醉和尚之溺于饮' means 'like the drunk monk's obsession with drinking', '而意不在饮' means 'but not in the drinking itself', and '则其笑亦可传已' means 'then his laughter can also be passed down'.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 许以八百金 - Offering or promising 800 taels of silver, a significant sum of money.
  • 天下莫贫于盐商 - Proverbial statement meaning 'there is no one poorer than a salt merchant,' reflecting the social and economic status of salt merchants.
  • 如数以钱置阶下 - Placing the exact amount of money at the foot of the steps.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening sentence introduces the offer and the response of the Taoist priest.
  • The proverbial statement sets the context for the social status of salt merchants.
  • This clause describes the condition of placing the money at the foot of the steps and taking it away after the task is completed.
  • This clause indicates a temporary acceptance of the condition to observe the outcome.
  • This clause describes the action of writing three talismans on yellow paper and burning them to pour over the woman.
  • This clause instructs to quickly prepare a chamber pot and warns of the imminent appearance of a malevolent force.
  • This clause describes the woman passing blood in the bucket and the appearance of the golden hairpin.
  • This clause identifies the gold as cursed and warns of further harm if not removed.
  • This clause suggests the removal and casting of the cursed gold into an image of a heavenly general to protect the family.
  • This clause describes the action of taking out the gold, washing it, and putting it in the sleeve.
  • This clause describes the action of slowly taking a small bag from the waist and blowing air into it.
  • This clause describes the action of the money disappearing into the bag in a moment, and it does not feel cramped.
  • This clause describes the action of tying the bag to the waist and looking at the gold while speaking.
  • This clause means 'I, the humble Taoist, have disturbed you today.'
  • This clause describes the action of raising the hand to express gratitude and then leaving.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 后王师袭绩溪，执督师御史金声 - Describes the Qing forces attacking Jixi and capturing the commander-in-chief and a censor named Jin Sheng.
  • 被杀时，洪承畴监斩，既死，尸不仆 - Describes the execution and the unusual state of Jin Sheng's body after death.
  • 洪入院，见声衣冠俨然危坐 - Describes how Hong Chengchou saw Jin Sheng sitting upright and dignified in his attire.
  • 恍惚不敢出者数日 - Describes Hong Chengchou's state of mind after the incident.
  • 此与瞿留守、张司马之身后现示者仿佛相似 - Compares Jin Sheng's posthumous manifestation to those of other historical figures.
  • 盖忠魂义魄，固当如河岳日星，不容掩抑也 - Explains why Jin Sheng's spirit could not be suppressed.
  • 外史氏曰 - Introduces the historian's commentary.
  • 余尝读沈廷芳《重修明兵部右侍郎左公祠碑铭》 - Introduces the historian's source material.
  • 后《自记》曰 - Introduces the subsequent section of the source material.
  • 顺治二年闰月二十日，公授命 - Provides the date and event of Jin Sheng's execution.
  • 是日莱阳乡人见公衣白衣，乘白驴，进南门至家 - Describes a supernatural event involving Jin Sheng.
  • 夫人刘淑人问公：“归来乎？” - Describes a conversation between Jin Sheng's wife and him.
  • 吾为兴朝所囚 - Jin Sheng's response to his wife.
  • 问以他事，则曰：“吾方可已乱矣。” - Describes Jin Sheng's response to other questions.
  • 时北窗下有木榻，公坐良久，乃去 - Describes Jin Sheng's actions after his conversation.
  • 其乡人仍见公由南门出 - Describes another supernatural event involving Jin Sheng.
  • 无何，懋泰遣人御公柩归矣 - Describes the escorting of Jin Sheng's coffin back home.
  • 越日，公所知从南来，云是日暮遇公于扬州，言欲往南京谒先帝，衣饰与所乘皆同 - Describes a report from people who knew Jin Sheng.
  • 盖公之忠诚，生死不忘君国如此 - Explains Jin Sheng's loyalty, never forgetting his king and country in life and death.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有楊生者，少年才俊 - A formal introduction of a young, talented man named Yang, typical of Classical Chinese narrative openings.

  • 娶婦某氏，慧麗絕倫 - Describes Yang's wife as exceptionally intelligent and beautiful, using hyperbolic language common in Classical Chinese.
  • 至是才逾年耳 - Means 'up to this point, it has only been a year,' indicating the short duration of their marriage.
  • 前一日，婦聞警，促生即往觅舟先遁 - Describes the wife's urgent action to save Yang, using a series of verbs to convey urgency and action.
  • 谓若待城破，将恐求死不得也 - Means 'if we wait until the city is captured, we may not be able to commit suicide,' reflecting the dire situation and the wife's determination.
  • 生恋家，未忍决去 - Indicates Yang's reluctance to leave his home, showing his emotional attachment.
  • 及夷匪至，始出觅舟，而满城大乱，舟已不可得 - Describes the arrival of the invaders and the ensuing chaos, making it impossible to find a boat.
  • 急返，闻妇哀号声彻外 - Means 'he rushed back, hearing his wife's cries resounding outside,' emphasizing the intensity of the situation.
  • 趨入，见黑鬼六七人，捽女发，将按淫焉 - Describes the invaders' violent actions, using vivid and graphic language.
  • 生跪为祈免，群匪怒，即捉生手足钉于门上 - Means 'Yang knelt down to beg for mercy, but the bandits became angry and immediately tied his hands and feet to the door,' showing the brutal response.
  • 旋捉女，褫其下衣，迭就淫之 - Describes the bandits' further violence against Yang's wife, using strong and explicit language.
  • 良久，宛转呼号而死，乃弃之 - Means 'after a long time, she writhed and cried out until she died, and then they left her,' indicating the prolonged suffering.
  • 后搜得仆妇数人，皆毙之而出 - Describes the bandits' subsequent actions, killing several servants.
  • 有老仆匿于床下，至是跃出，拔去其钉，抱生下 - Means 'an old servant hiding under the bed jumped out, removed the nails, and carried Yang down,' showing the servant's bravery.
  • 生不能起立，枕妇尸痛哭 - Means 'Yang could not stand up and wept over his wife's body,' expressing his deep grief.
  • 久之，蹒跚出门，意将觅死 - Means 'after a long time, he limped out of the door, intending to seek death,' indicating his despair.
  • 适遇白鬼数人，询知状，携生归 - Means 'he happened to meet several white ghosts, who asked about the situation and took him back,' suggesting a supernatural intervention.

  • 黑鬼 - Refers to the bandits, likely a derogatory term used to describe the invaders.
  • 白鬼 - Refers to the supernatural beings, possibly spirits or ghosts, reflecting the belief in the supernatural in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 认取黑鬼七人，杀之 - This part of the text suggests a supernatural element, where the wife's spirit takes revenge on the bandits, reflecting the belief in the afterlife and the possibility of spirits seeking justice.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有郭某者 - Nominal phrase introducing a person, 'There was a certain Guo.'
  • 汉奸 - Refers to a traitor to the Chinese nation, collaborating with foreign invaders.
  • 素为夷匪所倚 - '素' means 'always' or 'habitually,' '夷匪' refers to foreign bandits or invaders, and '所倚' means 'relied upon' or 'supported.' Together, it means 'who was always supported by foreign bandits.'
  • 掌兵权 - '掌' means 'hold' or 'control,' and '兵权' means 'military power' or 'command.' Together, it means 'who held military power.'
  • 犒以三十金 - '犒' means 'reward' or 'pay,' and '三十金' means 'thirty pieces of gold.' Together, it means 'rewarded with thirty pieces of gold.'
  • 俾另娶 - '俾' means 'to make' or 'to cause,' and '另娶' means 'to remarry.' Together, it means 'to make him remarry.'
  • 生携还 - '生' means 'he' or 'the man,' '携' means 'to carry' or 'to bring,' and '还' means 'back.' Together, it means 'he brought it back.'
  • 以其金命老仆往市两棺至 - '以其金' means 'with his money,' '命' means 'ordered,' '老仆' means 'old servant,' '往市' means 'to the market,' '两棺' means 'two coffins,' and '至' means 'arrived.' Together, it means 'he ordered his old servant to the market to buy two coffins.'
  • 将妇殓讫 - '将' means 'to prepare,' '妇' means 'wife,' '殓' means 'to wrap the body for burial,' and '讫' means 'finished.' Together, it means 'prepared his wife's body for burial.'
  • 长号数声 - '长号' means 'long wail,' and '数声' means 'several times.' Together, it means 'wailed several times.'
  • 以头触棺死 - '以头' means 'with his head,' '触' means 'to strike,' '棺' means 'coffin,' and '死' means 'died.' Together, it means 'struck his head against the coffin and died.'
  • 老仆即取空棺殓之 - '老仆' means 'old servant,' '即' means 'immediately,' '取' means 'to take,' '空棺' means 'empty coffin,' and '殓之' means 'wrapped him in it.' Together, it means 'the old servant immediately took an empty coffin and wrapped him in it.'
  • 而自缢焉 - '而' means 'and,' '自缢' means 'hanged himself,' and '焉' is a particle indicating completion. Together, it means 'and hanged himself.'
  • 其他遭其毒者 - '其他' means 'others,' '遭' means 'suffered,' '其毒' means 'his cruelty,' and '者' is a nominalizing particle. Together, it means 'others who suffered his cruelty.'
  • 亦不胜举 - '亦' means 'also,' '不胜举' means 'too numerous to count.' Together, it means 'are too numerous to count.'
  • 顷阅《扬州十日记》 - '顷' means 'recently,' '阅' means 'read,' and '《扬州十日记》' is the title of a historical text. Together, it means 'Recently read *The Ten Days of Yangzhou*.'
  • 历叙城破被难之苦 - '历叙' means 'chronologically narrate,' '城破' means 'the fall of the city,' '被难' means 'suffered calamity,' and '之苦' means 'the suffering.' Together, it means 'chronologically narrates the suffering of the people when the city fell.'
  • 令人不忍卒读 - '令人' means 'makes one,' '不忍' means 'unable to bear,' '卒读' means 'finish reading.' Together, it means 'makes one unable to finish reading.'
  • 乱离之际 - '乱离' means 'turmoil and exile,' and '之际' means 'at that time.' Together, it means 'at a time of turmoil and exile.'
  • 大体一辙也 - '大体' means 'in general,' '一辙' means 'the same,' and '也' is a particle indicating affirmation. Together, it means 'are generally the same.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 汉奸 - Refers to collaborators with foreign invaders during the period of Chinese history, particularly during the late Qing Dynasty and early Republican era.
  • 夷匪 - Refers to foreign bandits or invaders, often used to describe Western powers or Japanese forces during the late 19th and early 20th centuries.
  • 《扬州十日记》 - A historical text that chronicles the atrocities committed by the Qing army and their allies during the fall of Yangzhou in 1645. It is a significant source for understanding the suffering of civilians during the transition from the Ming to the Qing dynasty.
  • 乱离之际 - Refers to a time of political instability and social upheaval, often associated with wars, invasions, or revolutions.

  • 其健忘既有甚于虎 - Means 'their forgetfulness is even greater than that of a tiger,' using '虎' as a metaphor for forgetfulness or lack of memory.
  • 而其忍于横噬以杀人者，初不知所悔也 - Means 'and those who are cruel and kill people without remorse, do not even know regret,' where '横噬' is a metaphor for brutal and indiscriminate killing.
  • 呜呼！虎犹如此，奈何名之曰人，而反不如虎乎？ - Rhetorical expression meaning 'Alas! Even tigers are like this, how can we call them human and yet they are worse than tigers?' where '虎犹如此' emphasizes the severity of the situation.

  • The sentence uses a parallel structure to describe the size and reputation of the foreign ship.

  • The sentence uses a parallel structure to describe the ship's armament and its superiority over Chinese cannons.
  • The sentence uses a parallel structure to describe the public reaction and the foreign ship's intent.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '先是，翁以将宴客，召屠者宰猪，屡宰不绝，而又无血，及其他鸡鸭等物皆然' must be read as a single sequence of actions.
  • The clause '庖人所烹猪蹄，个个皆作殷红色，如涂鲜血' describes the unusual appearance of the cooked pig feet, which is a key detail in the narrative.
  • The clause '识者已共知为不祥，而翁犹迷而不知止，以致此误也' contrasts the wise people's recognition of the event as a bad omen with the master's failure to stop the activity.
  • The single word '惜哉' expresses regret or pity, often used to conclude a narrative or commentary.
  • The sequence '外史氏曰：此事余闻之丁子香。许汝樵亦在座，恻然曰：“此女固未庙见也，嫁之可矣。”' introduces a commentary and a dialogue, which must be read as a coherent unit.
  • The clause '余谓：即已庙见矣，已与某子合欢矣，而以十六七之红颜少妇，又无遗孤可抚，而必令其以寡鹄终也，于心安乎？' presents the narrator's opinion on the moral dilemma of forcing a young widow to remain unmarried.
  • The clause '然此女既已归婿门矣，此非如置器者，以不得其用，而遂可转售诸他人也。' argues against treating the woman like an object and reselling her.
  • The clause '况以今之世，虽在闺阁，皆喜矫立名义，甚有未婚而舆主迎娶，与殉其夫者' describes the current social attitudes and practices.
  • The clause '此固小儿女一时激烈之所为，君子所不愿见也，然而王法犹有所不禁也' expresses the moral disapproval of such actions, despite the lack of legal prohibition.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 昔者宋伯姬不肯下堂，以及于难，君子谓其女而不妇。 - The sequence of events (refusing to leave the hall, meeting disaster, and being called a daughter but not a wife) must be read as a logical progression.

  • 惟以暝夜与人交市，暗中嗅金，便知好恶。晓看皆如其言。 - The sentence describes a unique trading method, with 晓看皆如其言 confirming its accuracy.
  • 据《八纮译史》，乃罗刹国人也，在婆利之东。 - The sentence provides a source and location for the people described.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - A phrase used to introduce a commentator's or historian's remarks, similar to 'it is said' or 'the historian comments.'
  • 此《望溪文集》所纪黄公轶事 - Refers to the anecdotes of Mr. Huang recorded in the *Wangxi Wenji* (Collection of Wang Xi's Writings).
  • 与左忠毅公并书者也 - Indicates that these anecdotes are recorded alongside those of Left Zhongyi Gong, suggesting a parallel or joint account.
  • 古来忠臣义士 - Refers to loyal ministers and righteous scholars from ancient times.
  • 莫不以天下为己任 - A common phrase meaning 'none fail to take the world as their responsibility.'
  • 时丁板荡 - A literary expression meaning 'in times of turmoil' or 'during periods of chaos.'
  • 世际沧桑 - Refers to the vicissitudes of time, often translated as 'the world undergoes great changes.'
  • 犹将以一身力扶阳九 - A phrase meaning 'still strive to support the world single-handedly,' where '阳九' (yangjiu) is a term for a period of great calamity.
  • 不得已而以一死报国 - Means 'to die for the country when there is no other choice.'
  • 其意固以为未堪塞责也 - Indicates that their intention was to consider themselves unworthy of fulfilling their duties.
  • 从容授命 - Means 'to accept one's fate calmly and willingly.'
  • 忠义之名，有不忍言 - Refers to the reluctance to speak of the names of loyal and righteous individuals.
  • 余读佛书 - Means 'I have read Buddhist texts.'
  • 迦叶曰 - Refers to the statement made by Mahakasyapa, a disciple of the Buddha.
  • 金刚之身，非世间火所能烧 - A Buddhist phrase meaning 'the body of a Buddha is indestructible by worldly fire.'
  • 又《瑜伽论》曰 - Refers to the statement made in the *Yogacarabhumi-sastra* (Treatise on the Stages of Yoga).
  • 魔有四女，端正无伦 - Describes the four temptresses of Mara (the demon king) who are extremely beautiful.
  • 共来菩萨前，呈诸姿态 - Means 'they come before the Bodhisattva and display various postures.'
  • 菩萨以义心定力，四女皆变老丑，羞惭而退 - Indicates that the Bodhisattva, through his moral resolve, causes the four temptresses to appear old and ugly, leading them to retreat in shame.
  • 盖理之不胜夫欲 - Means 'the power of desire can overcome reason.'
  • 足令贲、育失其勇 - Refers to the idea that even the bravery of Guan Yu and Wu Zixu can be overcome by desire.
  • 良、平失其智 - Refers to the idea that even the wisdom of Zhang Liang and Zhuge Liang can be compromised by desire.
  • 惟仙、佛为能制之 - Indicates that only immortals and Buddhas can control desire.
  • 然仙佛一切不动 - Means 'however, immortals and Buddhas remain unmoved.'
  • 而圣贤则有动有静 - Indicates that sages and virtuous men have both active and passive states.
  • 以左公罹祸之惨 - Refers to the severe misfortune that befell Left Gong.
  • 凛凛数言 - Means 'a few solemn words.'
  • 至今犹有生气 - Indicates that these words still carry a sense of life and vitality.
  • 使其平居有如顾氏者 - Means 'if he had a person like Gu Shu in his daily life.'
  • 而与之键户同卧起 - Refers to living together in a closed room and sharing the same bed.
  • 谓能动其一顾哉 - Means 'could one expect to move him even slightly?'
  • 此先生发潜阐微意也 - Indicates that this is the commentator's intention to reveal hidden truths and subtle meanings.
  • 至黄公临命数语 - Refers to the final words of Mr. Huang before his death.
  • 分定固然 - Means 'destiny is predetermined.'
  • 亦二公之所同也 - Indicates that this is also true for both Left Gong and Mr. Huang.
  • 然此岂二公始念哉 - Means 'but was this their original intention?'
  • 此则可为二公痛哭者矣 - Indicates that this is something to mourn deeply for both Left Gong and Mr. Huang.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 按： - A note or annotation, often used to provide commentary or additional information.
  • 承畴之才，在明末诸臣中，似犹可任以兵事。 - This phrase suggests that although the source text does not provide specific details, it implies that the talent of a certain individual (Chengzhou) among the officials at the end of the Ming dynasty could have been suitable for military affairs.
  • 史中所纪战功，亦有可观者。 - This phrase indicates that the historical records of his military achievements are also noteworthy.
  • 然黄梨洲先生尝议其所叙战功之多诬，则有不可尽信者矣。 - This phrase suggests that Huang Lirou, a respected scholar, once criticized the exaggerated claims of his military achievements, indicating that they cannot be fully trusted.
  • 《檐曝杂记》言：承畴兵败时，其子弟在家，已刻行状散吊客。 - This phrase refers to a historical record that describes how, during Chengzhou's defeat, his family had already prepared and distributed his biography to mourners.
  • 崇祯帝方祭十四坛，而承畴生降之信至。 - This phrase indicates that while the Chongzhen Emperor was performing a ritual involving fourteen altars, news of Chengzhou's surrender reached him.
  • 后金声起兵徽州，与门人江天一俱败。 - This phrase describes how Jin Sheng, a later figure, raised an army in Huizhou but was defeated along with his disciple Jiang Tianyi.
  • 承畴谕令生降，天一诵御制祭文以愧之。 - This phrase suggests that Chengzhou ordered Jin Sheng to surrender, and Jiang Tianyi recited a royal decree to shame him.
  • 其后从本朝归没于京师，其子弟又刻行状，不复叙前朝事，即从本朝入关序起。 - This phrase indicates that after joining the new dynasty, Jiang Tianyi returned to the capital and died there, and his family prepared a new biography that did not mention his previous loyalty to the Ming dynasty, starting instead from his joining the new regime.
  • 有轻薄子得其两行状，订为一本，以作笑端云。 - This phrase suggests that someone with a light-hearted attitude compiled the two biographies into one book for amusement.
  • 《明史》又言：崇祯十四年，大清兵围松山。 - This phrase refers to another historical record that describes how, in the fourteenth year of the Chongzhen era, Qing forces besieged Songshan.
  • 承畴与邱民仰誓死固守，外援不至，刍粮并竭。 - This phrase describes how Chengzhou and Qiu Minyang swore to defend the city to the death, but without external support, their supplies of fodder and grain were depleted.
  • 至明年二月，已围半年矣。 - This phrase indicates that by the second month of the following year, the siege had lasted for half a year.
  • 力不支，城破，承畴降。 - This phrase describes how, due to exhaustion, the city was captured, and Chengzhou surrendered.
  • 民仰不屈，死，赠右副都御史，赐祭六坛，官为营葬，命建祠都城，与承畴并列。 - This phrase indicates that Qiu Minyang refused to surrender and was killed, posthumously receiving the title of Right Deputy Censor-in-Chief, being honored with six altars, and having a temple built for him in the capital alongside Chengzhou.
  • 帝将亲临致祭，后闻承畴降，乃止。 - This phrase suggests that the emperor intended to personally attend the memorial service but stopped upon hearing that Chengzhou had surrendered.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此说在明野史中，俱未之及 - Formal expression meaning 'This account is not mentioned in the wild histories of the Ming dynasty.'
  • 吾谓李公映碧《三垣笔记》极言襄愍临刑之惨，与此不符 - Means 'I believe that Li Gong Yingbi's *Three Halls Notes* strongly describes the tragic execution of Xiang Min, which does not match this account.'
  • 然陈氏乃亲见者，当不诬也 - Means 'However, Chen Shi was a direct witness, so it is unlikely to be false.'
  • 史稿但纪襄愍保辽之功，而不言其通术数 - Means 'The draft history only records Xiang Min's achievements in protecting Liaodong, but does not mention his knowledge of divination.'
  • 惟于万历三十五年巡按辽东时，岁大旱，行部金州，祷城隍神，约三日不雨，毁其庙 - Means 'However, during his inspection of Liaodong in the 35th year of Wanli, there was a severe drought. He traveled to Jinzhou, prayed to the City God, and vowed to destroy the temple if it did not rain within three days.'
  • 及至广宁，逾三日，大书白牌，封剑，使使往斩之 - Means 'When he reached Guangning, after three days, he wrote a large white notice, sealed his sword, and sent someone to destroy the temple.'
  • 未至，风雷大作，雨如注 - Means 'Before they arrived, strong winds and thunderstorms broke out, and it rained heavily.'
  • 辽人以为神 - Means 'The people of Liaodong considered it a divine intervention.'
  • 据此，则狱中之事自非无稽也 - Means 'Based on this, the events in prison are not groundless.'
  • 五月初六日，哈哒门火神庙庙祝见火神飒飒行动，势将下殿，忙拈香跪告曰 - Means 'On the sixth day of May, the temple priest of the Fire God Temple in Hadamen saw the Fire God moving vigorously, as if descending from the hall, and hurriedly offered incense and knelt to say...'
  • 火神举足欲出，庙祝哀哭抱住 - Means 'The Fire God lifted his foot to step out, and the temple priest cried in sorrow and held him back.'
  • 方推阻间，而震声旋举矣 - Means 'Just as they were pushing and resisting, the sound of the earthquake suddenly arose.'
  • 有一绍兴周吏目之弟，因兄荣选 - Means 'There was a younger brother of a Zhou magistrate from Shaoxing, who was selected due to his brother's honor.'
  • 思做公弟，到京方三日，从菜市口买一蓝纱褶，摇摆而还 - Means 'He wanted to be a public servant, and had only been in the capital for three days when he bought a blue silk jacket from Caishikou and returned, swaying.'
  • 途遇六人，拜揖尚未完，头忽飞去，陷入墙内寸许 - Means 'On the way, he met six people. They had not finished bowing and greeting when his head suddenly flew off and sank into the wall by an inch.'
  • 眉毛又粘在一处 - Means 'His eyebrows also stuck together.'
  • 其六人者无恙 - Means 'The six people were unharmed.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 王秋泉者，吾邑名医也 - Formal introduction, 'Mr. Qiuquan, a renowned physician in our county.'
  • 延秋泉 - 'Invited Mr. Qiuquan,' where '延' means 'to invite' or 'to summon.'
  • 不果往 - 'Could not go,' indicating failure to fulfill an obligation.
  • 中夜绵惙 - 'In the middle of the night, he was in great distress,' where '绵惙' describes a state of deep worry or anxiety.
  • 宁得一当王先生，死不恨 - 'If I could see Mr. Qiuquan once before dying, I would not regret it,' expressing a strong desire to meet the doctor.
  • 顿首敦促 - 'Knelt and urged earnestly,' where '顿首' is a formal bow, and '敦促' means 'to urge.'
  • 会所治贵人疾良巳 - 'Just as the illness of the nobleman he was treating had improved,' where '良巳' means 'improved' or 'recovered.'
  • 又数日贵人起 - 'After several more days, the nobleman recovered,' indicating a full recovery.
  • 治具觞秋泉 - 'Prepared a feast to entertain Mr. Qiuquan,' where '治具' means 'to prepare a meal,' and '觞' means 'to entertain with wine.'
  • 奉金币为寿 - 'Presented gold and coins as a gift for longevity,' where '金币' refers to valuable gifts, and '为寿' means 'to wish for a long life.'
  • 秋泉饮大醉归 - 'Mr. Qiuquan drank until he was thoroughly drunk and returned home,' where '大醉' means 'thoroughly drunk.'
  • 归至舟中，语家人曰 - 'When he returned to his boat, he told his family,' where '语家人' means 'told his family.'
  • 今可赴富人约矣 - 'Now I can keep my appointment with the rich man,' where '赴约' means 'to keep an appointment.'
  • 抵其家，传呼曰 - 'When he arrived at the man's house, he shouted out,' where '传呼' means 'to shout out.'
  • 举家惊喜出迎 - 'The entire family was surprised and joyfully came out to greet him,' where '举家' means 'the entire family,' and '惊喜' means 'surprised and joyful.'
  • 秋泉方酣睡，家人起诸梦中 - 'Mr. Qiuquan was sound asleep, and his family woke him from his dream,' where '方酣睡' means 'sound asleep,' and '起诸梦中' means 'woke him from his dream.'
  • 主人已盛衣冠，鞠躬入舟肃客 - 'The host had already put on his finest clothes and bowed respectfully to welcome Mr. Qiuquan into the boat,' where '盛衣冠' means 'put on his finest clothes,' and '鞠躬' means 'bowed respectfully.'
  • 主人固请曰 - 'The host insisted, saying,' where '固请' means 'insisted.'
  • 老父忍死待先生，先生幸辱临，何栉沐为 - 'My father has been waiting to see you, clinging to life. Please honor us by coming, why should you bother to bathe and groom yourself?' where '忍死' means 'clinging to life,' and '幸辱临' means 'honor us by coming.'
  • 强之入 - 'He forced him to come in,' where '强之入' means 'forced him to come in.'
  • 诊脉已，与药竟出 - 'After diagnosing his pulse and giving him medicine, he left,' where '诊脉已' means 'after diagnosing his pulse,' and '与药竟出' means 'gave him medicine and left.'
  • 主人盛馔揖秋泉 - 'The host prepared a feast and bowed to Mr. Qiuquan,' where '盛馔' means 'prepared a feast,' and '揖' means 'bowed.'
  • 秋泉但摇手谢 - 'Mr. Qiuquan merely waved his hand and thanked him,' where '摇手' means 'waved his hand.'
  • 鸡鸣酒醒，呼其家人骂曰 - 'When he woke up from his drunken sleep, he called his family and scolded them,' where '鸡鸣酒醒' means 'woke up from his drunken sleep,' and '呼其家人骂曰' means 'called his family and scolded them.'
  • 惰奴旷乃公事 - 'Lazy servant, neglecting your duties,' where '惰奴' means 'lazy servant,' and '旷乃公事' means 'neglecting your duties.'
  • 且某富人迟我久，当夜赴之，何尚泊此 - 'Moreover, the rich man has been waiting for me for a long time, I should have gone to him at night, why are we still here?' where '迟我久' means 'has been waiting for me for a long time,' and '何尚泊此' means 'why are we still here?'
  • 顷已诊脉与药，忘之耶 - 'We already diagnosed his pulse and gave him medicine, have you forgotten?' where '顷已诊脉与药' means 'already diagnosed his pulse and gave him medicine,' and '忘之耶' means 'have you forgotten?'
  • 审与药乎 - 'Did you really give him medicine?' where '审与药乎' means 'did you really give him medicine?'
  • 吾真大醉，必杀之矣 - 'I was truly drunk, I must have killed him,' where '真大醉' means 'truly drunk,' and '必杀之矣' means 'must have killed him.'
  • 顿足，促解维归 - 'He stamped his foot and hurriedly ordered them to untie the boat and return,' where '顿足' means 'stamped his foot,' and '促解维归' means 'hurriedly ordered them to untie the boat and return.'
  • 谓不去必受辱 - 'Saying that if they did not leave, they would be disgraced,' where '谓不去必受辱' means 'saying that if they did not leave, they would be disgraced.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王秋泉 - A renowned physician in the local area, reflecting the importance of medical practitioners in traditional Chinese society.
  • 富人 - A wealthy individual, highlighting the social hierarchy and the role of wealth in accessing medical care.
  • 贵人 - A noble or high-ranking official, emphasizing the social status and the respect accorded to such individuals.
  • 治具觞秋泉 - Preparing a feast to entertain a guest, a common practice in traditional Chinese hospitality.
  • 奉金币为寿 - Presenting valuable gifts as a gesture of respect and gratitude, a traditional way to honor guests or benefactors.
  • 盛衣冠，鞠躬入舟肃客 - Putting on fine clothes and bowing respectfully, reflecting the importance of proper etiquette and respect in social interactions.
  • 忍死待先生 - Clinging to life to see a respected physician, highlighting the high regard for medical expertise in traditional Chinese culture.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 王秋泉者，吾邑名医也 - This is a formal introduction, but it could also be read as a statement of fact about Mr. Qiuquan's status.
  • 中夜绵惙，谓其子曰 - The sentence structure is compact, and the emotional state of the father is emphasized through the phrase '绵惙.'
  • 顿首敦促 - The phrase is elliptical, but it clearly indicates the urgency and respect of the request.
  • 会所治贵人疾良巳，又数日贵人起 - This sequence of events is tightly linked, showing the improvement and recovery of the nobleman.
  • 治具觞秋泉，奉金币为寿 - The sentence is a compound structure, linking the preparation of a feast with the presentation of gifts.
  • 秋泉饮大醉归，归至舟中，语家人曰 - The sequence of events is clear, but the emotional state of Mr. Qiuquan is emphasized through his drunkenness.
  • 抵其家，传呼曰 - The sentence is elliptical, but it clearly indicates the arrival and the reaction of the host's family.
  • 举家惊喜出迎 - The phrase is a compound structure, linking the family's reaction to their joy and surprise.
  • 秋泉方酣睡，家人起诸梦中 - The sentence is elliptical, but it clearly indicates the state of Mr. Qiuquan and the action of his family.
  • 主人已盛衣冠，鞠躬入舟肃客 - The sentence is a compound structure, linking the host's preparation with his respectful behavior.

  • 外史氏曰 - A conventional phrase used to introduce a commentary or reflection, often by a historian or scholar.

  • 交趾金溪究山 - Refers to a specific location in ancient China, likely in the region of Jiaozhi (modern-day northern Vietnam).

  • 長十丈，圍七八尺 - Provides a vivid description of the snake's size, emphasizing its enormity.
  • 常在樹上伺鹿獸 - Describes the snake's behavior of waiting in trees to ambush deer or other animals.

  • 便食頭角 - Describes the snake's consumption of the prey's head and horns.
  • 三月骨皆鑽皮出 - Suggests that after three months, the bones of the prey emerge through the snake's skin.
  • 山夷始見蛇不動時 - Refers to the local people (山夷) seeing the snake when it is motionless.
  • 以竹簽簽蛇頭至尾 - Describes the method of killing the snake by inserting a bamboo stake from head to tail.

  • 以為珍異 - Suggests that the snake is considered a delicacy or something rare and valuable.
  • 一說以婦人衣投之 - Refers to an alternative method of capturing the snake by throwing a woman's garment at it.
  • 則蟠而不起走 - Describes the snake's reaction of coiling up and remaining still instead of fleeing.

  • 楊氏南裔《異物志》 - Refers to a work titled *Yì Wù Zhì* (Record of Strange Things) by Yang, likely a southern scholar.
  • 蚺惟大蛇，既宏且長 - Describes the *rán shé* as a large and long snake.
  • 采色驳荦 - Refers to the snake's varied and colorful appearance.
  • 其文錦章 - Describes the snake's skin as having a pattern like brocade.
  • 食猪吞鹿 - Indicates that the snake eats pigs and swallows deer whole.
  • 腴成養創 - Suggests that the snake's fat is used to heal wounds.
  • 賓享嘉宴，是豆是觞 - Refers to the snake's fat being served at banquets as a delicacy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - A title often used by historians or scholars to introduce their commentary, similar to 'the historian says.'
  • 《烏青志》 - A historical or local chronicle, possibly a regional record of events or legends.
  • 《水經注》 - A classic geographical and historical work by Li Daoyuan, which provides detailed descriptions of rivers and related legends.
  • 交趾金溪究山 - A specific location in ancient China, likely in the region of Jiaozhi (modern-day northern Vietnam).
  • 山夷 - Refers to the local people or indigenous inhabitants of the area, often used to describe non-Han ethnic groups.
  • 《異物志》 - A work that records strange or unusual creatures, often found in ancient texts.
  • 楊氏 - Likely refers to a southern scholar or author, possibly from the southern regions of China.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This phrase introduces the source of the story but does not specify the exact nature of the event.
  • This sentence combines a surreal experience with a description of the miraculous effects of medicine, creating a contrast between the real and the fantastical.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乾隆辛丑十月 - Refers to October 1751 (the year of the ox in the sexagenary cycle) during the Qing dynasty.
  • 永城尉 - Refers to an official position in Yongcheng, a county in Henan province.
  • 署中旧有柳树一枝 - Means 'there was an old willow tree in the courtyard.'
  • 年久半槁 - Describes the willow tree as 'half-dead from age.'
  • 命工伐之 - Means 'ordered workers to cut it down.'
  • 其中纹画如淡墨写成 - Describes the wood grain as 'patterned like ink wash painting.'
  • 左右峰石峻削 - Refers to 'sharp, steep rocks on both sides.'
  • 悬崖之上有松一株 - Means 'a pine tree on a cliff.'
  • 藤缠累累 - Describes 'vines entwined around the pine.'
  • 老树一株 - Refers to 'an old tree.'
  • 枝叶皆倒垂 - Describes 'branches and leaves hanging downward.'
  • 下有一叟 - Means 'below is an old man.'
  • 挟杖立 - Describes 'holding a staff and standing.'
  • 高冠长袖 - Refers to 'a tall hat and long sleeves.'
  • 须眉宛然 - Means 'beard and eyebrows are clearly visible.'
  • 其左手纳袖中，著胸前 - Describes 'his left hand in his sleeve, resting on his chest.'
  • 右脚前行露其舄 - Means 'his right foot forward, showing his shoes.'
  • 左脚隐衣下 - Describes 'his left foot hidden under his robe.'
  • 回顾若听泉状 - Means 'looking back as if listening to a stream.'
  • 虽妙手写生，不是过也 - Means 'even a master painter could not surpass this.'
  • 造物之巧，岂可思哉 - Means 'the ingenuity of creation, how can it be conceived!'
  • 康熙壬寅 - Refers to the year 1672 (the year of the tiger in the sexagenary cycle) during the Qing dynasty.
  • 京口檄造战舰 - Means 'an order was issued in Jingkou to build warships.'
  • 江都刘氏园中 - Refers to 'in the garden of Liu's family in Jiangdu.'
  • 百余年物也 - Means 'a tree over a hundred years old.'
  • 亦被伐 - Means 'it was also cut down.'
  • 及锯开 - Means 'when it was sawn open.'
  • 木之立理有观音大士像二 - Describes 'two images of Guanyin Bodhisattva in the wood grain.'
  • 妙曼天然 - Means 'graceful and natural.'
  • 众共骇异 - Means 'everyone was amazed.'
  • 乃施之持南福缘庵中 - Means 'they were placed in the Southern Fortune Temple.'
  • 此似有神物凭之者 - Means 'this seems to be the work of a divine presence.'
  • 尤奇 - Means 'especially strange.'
